growth_orientation_rationale: The subsidies are designed to promote renewable energy production, which can lead to long-term environmental benefits and economic growth through the development of a competitive wind energy sector.
status_evidence: The subsidies have been in place since 1992 and have significantly supported the growth of the wind energy sector.
importance_rationale: The reform represents a major commitment to renewable energy, significantly impacting the energy sector and contributing to Denmark's leadership in wind energy technology.
description: Denmark has implemented substantial public subsidies to support the wind turbine sector, which includes production subsidies, tax subsidies for co-operatively-owned wind turbines, and guaranteed prices for wind-generated electricity.
source_quote: The high cost of producing wind turbine electricity has required substantial public subsidies to enable the sector to expand, totalling almost DKr4 billion over the past six years.

growth_orientation_rationale: Facilitating access to funding for innovative startups is expected to enhance entrepreneurship and innovation, contributing positively to economic growth.
status_evidence: Both initiatives have been successfully established and are operational.
importance_rationale: The establishment of a risk capital market and a permanent support scheme represents significant but not structural changes to the entrepreneurial ecosystem.
description: The Danish government established a risk capital market for entrepreneurs and made an entrepreneurial support scheme permanent, aimed at enhancing the entrepreneurial climate and addressing market failures in the supply of risk capital to SMEs, facilitating access to funding for innovative startups.
source_quote: An entrepreneurial support scheme has been made permanent, and has been followed by government intervention to establish a market for subordinate loan capital to enterprises.

growth_orientation_rationale: Enhancing competition and innovation in the rail transport sector is expected to improve efficiency and service quality, contributing to economic growth.
status_evidence: The framework has been established and is currently in effect.
importance_rationale: The establishment of a framework for competition and innovation represents a significant change in the governance of the rail transport sector.
description: The Danish government established a framework for improving competition in the rail transport sector, which includes measures to enhance innovation in the sector.
source_quote: Parliament adopts a framework for improving competition in the rail transport sector.

growth_orientation_rationale: The tax credits incentivize private sector investment in R&D, potentially leading to increased innovation and productivity.
status_evidence: The competition package was introduced in January 2002.
importance_rationale: The introduction of tax credits for R&D represents a significant policy change aimed at enhancing private sector innovation, although it is part of a broader regulatory simplification effort.
description: A competition package was introduced to simplify regulation and ease firms’ administrative burdens, including a pilot scheme on tax credits for business R&D investments for the years 2002 and 2003.
source_quote: A competition package was introduced in January 2002 to simplify regulation and ease firms’ administrative burdens.

growth_orientation_rationale: Simplifying tax rules and providing tax credits are expected to increase private investment in innovation, thereby supporting economic growth.
status_evidence: The tax credit scheme has been introduced and is operational.
importance_rationale: These reforms are significant in promoting private investment but do not represent a complete overhaul of the system.
description: The Danish government is working to simplify complicated tax rules that hinder private investment in innovation and venture capital, while also having introduced a tax credit scheme to encourage collaboration between firms and public research institutions. These measures aim to enhance research partnerships and stimulate private sector investment in innovation.
source_quote: A tax credit scheme was tried out in 2002 whereby a firm received a 150% tax credit for payments made to a public institution as part of a collaborative research project.

growth_orientation_rationale: Enhancing technology transfer and commercialization is expected to lead to increased innovation and economic growth.
status_evidence: The reform has been put into practice, allowing universities to claim ownership of inventions.
importance_rationale: The reform significantly changes the ownership structure of inventions, which is a major shift in policy.
description: Denmark's government has implemented a reform allowing universities to claim ownership of their employees' inventions, which enhances their ability to commercialize intellectual property and boost technology transfer. This reform aims to improve technology transfer to enhance the commercialization of research, addressing previous bureaucratic obstacles.
source_quote: A reform in 2000 improved technology transfer, but research funding is still largely institution-based with little explicit link to quality.

growth_orientation_rationale: Increasing R&D spending and implementing performance-based funding are expected to enhance research quality and relevance, contributing to economic growth.
status_evidence: R&D spending has increased, and the government is actively working on the funding mechanism.
importance_rationale: The shift to performance-based funding is significant but does not represent a complete overhaul of the funding system.
description: Denmark has seen an increase in R&D spending, indicating a commitment to enhancing its research capabilities and innovation environment. The government plans to shift towards a contestable performance-based funding mechanism for research, allowing private firms to compete for public research funds on equal terms, which is expected to improve the effectiveness of the research budget by tying funding to quality outcomes.
source_quote: Getting better results from the research budget would also be useful. One way is to shift further towards a contestable performance-based funding mechanism and to tie funding to quality.

growth_orientation_rationale: Easing bankruptcy rules can help preserve viable businesses and promote innovation by allowing them to recover.
status_evidence: The reform has been successfully implemented since 2005.
importance_rationale: The reform is an adjustment to existing rules rather than a major overhaul.
description: Bankruptcy rules have been eased to allow greater opportunities for informal corporate rescue plans, facilitating smoother transitions for struggling businesses.
source_quote: Bankruptcy rules were eased October 2005.

growth_orientation_rationale: Encouraging pension funds to invest in venture capital is expected to enhance funding for innovative startups.
status_evidence: The reform has been enacted and is currently in effect.
importance_rationale: The reform is significant as it modifies tax rules to stimulate investment in innovation.
description: Taxation rules for pension funds have been revised to encourage their participation in the venture capital market, including a temporary 5% tax relief for investments via SME market places.
source_quote: Taxation of pension funds has been simplified from January 2006.

growth_orientation_rationale: The impact on economic growth is ambiguous as it focuses on social issues rather than direct innovation.
status_evidence: The measures have been enacted and are currently in effect.
importance_rationale: The reform addresses significant social challenges but does not directly relate to innovation policy.
description: The Danish government has launched several measures to improve integration and reduce ghetto problems, including renovating and modernising buildings in the social housing sector and earmarking funds for social initiatives.
source_quote: The government has recently launched several measures to cope with integration and ghetto problems.

growth_orientation_rationale: By enhancing skills and promoting lifelong learning, the strategy supports the development of a more capable workforce, which is essential for innovation.
status_evidence: The initiatives outlined in the Globalisation Strategy have been put into practice since 2006.
importance_rationale: This reform represents a major initiative to align education and training with the needs of the economy, significantly impacting human capital development.
description: Denmark's Globalisation Strategy from 2006 aims to enhance R&D and higher education financing, promoting lifelong learning and skill development in the public sector. This includes systematic projections of future staffing and skill needs, and expanding vocational training for social and health assistants.
source_quote: In June 2007, a major agreement was entered between the government, the local/regional authorities and the unions covering virtually all parts of the public sector workforce.

growth_orientation_rationale: The reform is expected to enhance public sector efficiency, which can lead to better allocation of resources and improved service delivery.
status_evidence: A major structural reform will come into force from January 2007.
importance_rationale: This reform represents a significant overhaul of local governance structures, which is expected to have lasting impacts on public administration efficiency and service delivery.
description: A major structural reform will come into force from January 2007, reducing the number of municipalities from 271 to 98 and replacing the 14 counties by 5 regions, aimed at improving public sector efficiency.
source_quote: A major structural reform will come into force from January 2007 reducing the number of municipalities from 271 to 98 and replacing the 14 counties by 5 regions.

growth_orientation_rationale: Increased funding for R&D is expected to enhance innovation and competitiveness in various sectors.
status_evidence: The funding has been allocated and utilized for various R&D initiatives.
importance_rationale: This significant investment in R&D is a major reform aimed at enhancing the country's innovation capacity.
description: The Danish government announced investments of an additional €1.5 billion in R&D over the period of 2007-2010 to boost research and innovation activities.
source_quote: The government announced investments of an additional €1.5 billion in R&D over 2007-10.

growth_orientation_rationale: Employer-financed vocational training is expected to enhance the skills of the workforce, contributing to innovation and productivity.
status_evidence: The agreement has been put into practice and is currently active.
importance_rationale: While significant, this agreement is more of an incremental adjustment rather than a structural reform.
description: A new three-year collective employment agreement for the manufacturing sector was negotiated in 2007, which includes a commitment to employer-financed vocational training, allowing employees to receive training while earning a portion of their wages.
source_quote: A new three-year collective employment agreement for the manufacturing sector... contained a new commitment to employer-financed vocational training.

growth_orientation_rationale: By providing funding for basic research, the fund supports foundational knowledge creation, which is essential for long-term innovation.
status_evidence: The Financing Fund has been established and is operational since 2008.
importance_rationale: This fund is significant for supporting basic research but does not represent a major overhaul of the funding system.
description: The Financing Fund was created to support basic research in Denmark, managing around DKK1.5 billion in assets.
source_quote: The Financing Fund manages around DKK1.5 billion in assets that are to be used to support basic research in Denmark.

growth_orientation_rationale: This fund aims to improve infrastructure, which is crucial for economic development and efficiency.
status_evidence: The fund has been operational since its creation, financing various infrastructure projects.
importance_rationale: The establishment of a large fund for infrastructure is a significant reform with broad economic implications.
description: The Danish government created a DKK90 billion fund to finance infrastructure projects over 2009-2020, addressing a significant infrastructure backlog and prioritizing investments based on socio-economic returns.
source_quote: The government created a DKK90 billion fund to finance infrastructure projects over 2009-20, including in environment and public transport.

growth_orientation_rationale: Improving the business environment is expected to enhance entrepreneurship and innovation.
status_evidence: The reforms have been enacted and are currently in effect.
importance_rationale: This reform package represents a significant overhaul of the business operating environment.
description: The Danish government unveiled a new package of reforms aimed at improving conditions for starting and operating businesses, which includes measures to facilitate access to authorities and streamline administrative processes.
source_quote: In March 2009, the government unveiled a new package of reforms to that effect, including providing better conditions for starting and operating businesses.

growth_orientation_rationale: Encouraging timely completion of tertiary education is expected to enhance the supply of skilled researchers and innovators.
status_evidence: The proposal has been made but not yet implemented.
importance_rationale: The reform is significant in promoting educational outcomes but is not a major overhaul.
description: The Danish government proposed adjustments to public study grants to encourage early entry and on-schedule completion of tertiary education, including cutting monthly grants for late starters and offering bonuses for academic progress.
source_quote: To create such incentives, the government has proposed a set of adjustments of the public study grants as part of the welfare reform currently being discussed.

growth_orientation_rationale: Enhancing financial options for small and medium-sized companies supports their growth and innovation potential.
status_evidence: The scheme has been actively in place since its boost in 2009-10.
importance_rationale: The reform significantly boosts financial options for SMEs, which is crucial for their development.
description: A loan guarantee scheme available to all firms has been boosted in 2009-10, aimed at enhancing financial options for small and medium-sized companies.
source_quote: A loan guarantee scheme available to all firms has been boosted in 2009-10.

growth_orientation_rationale: This reform enhances financial support for R&D activities, particularly for young firms, thereby fostering innovation and productivity growth.
status_evidence: The scheme was introduced to support R&D spending among firms.
importance_rationale: This reform represents a significant change in the support structure for R&D, particularly for small and young firms, which is crucial for fostering innovation and economic growth.
description: The Danish government introduced a new scheme allowing firms that spend on R&D and face after-tax income losses to benefit from a tax refund of 25% of these losses, aimed at improving access to funding for young and financially constrained firms.
source_quote: In 2012, a new tax incentives scheme was introduced, allowing firms that spend on R&D and face after-tax income losses to benefit from a tax refund of 25% of these losses.

growth_orientation_rationale: Enhances collaboration and knowledge sharing, which can lead to increased innovation and competitiveness among SMEs.
status_evidence: The new cluster strategy was launched in April 2013.
importance_rationale: This reform represents a significant structural change aimed at enhancing innovation through improved cluster dynamics, which is crucial for the competitiveness of the Danish economy.
description: A new cluster strategy was launched to improve framework conditions for innovation and knowledge sharing, focusing on expanding clusters and increasing collaboration at various levels.
source_quote: In April 2013, a new cluster strategy was launched to improve framework conditions for innovation and knowledge sharing, with a view to expanding clusters.

growth_orientation_rationale: By incentivizing quicker completion of tertiary education, the reform aims to increase the skilled labor supply, which can enhance productivity and economic growth.
status_evidence: The reform was enacted in April 2013.
importance_rationale: This reform represents a significant overhaul of the grant system, aiming to improve educational outcomes and labor market readiness, which is crucial for economic competitiveness.
description: In April 2013, the state grant system for tertiary education was reformed to speed up completion and thereby raise labour supply, introducing stricter conditions for grant eligibility and shortening the average duration of study programmes.
source_quote: In April 2013, the state grant system for tertiary education was reformed to speed up completion and thereby raise labour supply.

growth_orientation_rationale: Facilitating innovation in SMEs is expected to enhance competition and drive economic growth.
status_evidence: The programme has been put into action and is currently operational.
importance_rationale: The programme represents a significant initiative to support SMEs in innovation, indicating a notable but not transformative change.
description: The new InnoBooster programme is designed to facilitate innovation in SMEs, ensuring sufficient competition and SME participation in demand-side innovation policies.
source_quote: The new InnoBooster programme is designed to facilitate innovation in SMEs.

growth_orientation_rationale: The Council aims to harness technological changes for broad economic benefits, potentially enhancing productivity and innovation across sectors.
status_evidence: The Council was established and is actively functioning.
importance_rationale: The establishment of the Council represents a significant step in coordinating responses to technological disruption, fostering collaboration among various stakeholders.
description: The Danish Government launched a 'Disruption Council' to analyze and discuss future challenges from technological changes and globalization, aiming to ensure that all Danes benefit from economic development.
source_quote: In May 2017, the Danish Government established a 'Disruption Council', a partnership between the social partners, businesses, experts and the Government.

growth_orientation_rationale: The reform aims to address market failures by providing better access to capital and guidance for startups, which can enhance innovation and firm growth.
status_evidence: The reform was approved and implemented to simplify the system.
importance_rationale: The simplification of the business promotion services is significant as it enhances the support structure for startups, which is crucial for fostering innovation and economic growth.
description: In 2018, Denmark approved a reform to simplify the public business promotion services system, enhancing support for startups and medium-term firm growth.
source_quote: In 2018, a welcome reform was approved to simplify the system significantly.

growth_orientation_rationale: Enhancing cybersecurity is expected to build trust in digital technologies, which is essential for their adoption and the overall productivity growth of firms.
status_evidence: The Government launched a national strategy for cyber and information security.
importance_rationale: The reform addresses a critical area of concern for businesses, particularly small firms, and aims to enhance their resilience against cyber threats, which is vital for fostering innovation.
description: The 2018 Digital Growth reform package includes initiatives to strengthen cybersecurity in companies, establishing a single digital point of entry for reporting IT security incidents and launching a national strategy for cyber and information security.
source_quote: The 2018 Digital Growth reform package contains a number of initiatives to strengthen cyber security in companies.

growth_orientation_rationale: Increasing the number of STEM graduates will enhance the skill base necessary for innovation and technological advancement.
status_evidence: The Government launched a Technology Pact aiming to raise the number of STEM graduates.
importance_rationale: This initiative is significant in addressing skill shortages in STEM fields, which are critical for future innovation.
description: The Government launched a Technology Pact aiming to raise the number of STEM graduates in collaboration with companies, educational and research institutions.
source_quote: In April 2018, the Government also launched a Technology Pact aiming to raise the number of STEM graduates in collaboration with companies.

growth_orientation_rationale: Encouraging timely completion of education and increasing competitive research funding are expected to enhance human capital and innovation capacity.
component_name: Tertiary Education and R&D Funding Reform
status_evidence: The measures have been put into practice as part of ongoing government initiatives.
importance_rationale: The reforms are significant but not structural, focusing on enhancing education and competitive funding mechanisms.
description: Denmark is implementing measures to encourage timely completion of tertiary education, including making it easier for students to transition from secondary to tertiary education and providing universities with incentives to promote on-time completion. Additionally, the government has been working towards increasing the share of public research grants allocated on a competitive basis, aiming for at least 50% of funds to be subject to open competition, alongside investments in R&D in the agriculture sector to support green growth.
source_quote: The government reached agreement with its parliamentary partners in June 2009 on a 'green growth' initiative with new measures focused on improving the environmental performance of the agriculture sector.

growth_orientation_rationale: Increasing tax incentives for R&D is expected to stimulate private sector investment in innovation, particularly benefiting young and innovative firms.
status_evidence: The enhanced tax allowance rate will gradually increase as planned.
importance_rationale: This reform is a significant adjustment to the existing tax incentive framework, aimed at increasing R&D investment in the private sector.
description: Denmark is set to increase R&D tax incentives by introducing an enhanced tax allowance for current R&D expenditure, gradually increasing from 1.5% in 2019 to 10% in 2026.
source_quote: Recent reform will increase R&D tax incentives by introducing enhanced tax allowance for current R&D expenditure.

growth_orientation_rationale: Improving the education system aligns skills with market demand, enhancing productivity and innovation potential.
status_evidence: An agreement was passed in December 2017 to reform university funding.
importance_rationale: This reform represents a structural change in how university funding is allocated, which is crucial for aligning education with future labor market needs.
description: The Government has initiated a wide scale reflection on how to improve the education system and make it more relevant for the labour market by reforming the university funding system and better targeting labour market demand and future skill needs in education.
source_quote: An agreement was passed in December 2017 to reform university funding based on quality and outcomes of students.

growth_orientation_rationale: The strategy supports innovation in clean technologies, enhancing sustainability and economic growth.
status_evidence: Programmes like EUDP are already in operation.
importance_rationale: The strategy represents a major overhaul of Denmark's approach to clean technology R&D.
description: Denmark is implementing a comprehensive strategy to support research and development in clean technologies, aiming to cut carbon emissions by 70% by 2030. This includes attracting private investment, enhancing public-private cooperation, and focusing on missions such as carbon capture, power-to-X, and eco-friendly agriculture. Major programmes like the Energy Technology Development and Demonstration Programme (EUDP) are central to this effort.
source_quote: The green research strategy is focused on four missions: 1) carbon capture, utilisation and storage, 2) power-to-X, 3) climate and eco-friendly agriculture and food production, 4) recycling and reduction of plastic waste.

growth_orientation_rationale: The tax incentive encourages private sector investment in R&D, boosting innovation.
status_evidence: The tax allowance was applied in 2020 and extended.
importance_rationale: The temporary increase is a significant incentive for private R&D investment.
description: Denmark temporarily increased the R&D enhanced tax allowance to 130% for R&D expenses up to DKK 850 million in 2020 and 2021, later extended to 2022.
source_quote: The package also included a temporary increase in the R&D enhanced tax allowance to 130% in 2020 and 2021 (later extended to 2022) for R&D expenses up to DKK 850 million.

growth_orientation_rationale: Integrating digital technologies in healthcare is expected to improve service delivery and efficiency, contributing positively to economic growth.
status_evidence: The political agreement has been reached and initiatives are underway to implement the integration of digital technologies in healthcare.
importance_rationale: The agreement represents a significant step towards improving healthcare services through technology, though it is not a complete overhaul.
description: A political agreement was reached to strengthen the integration of healthcare services and develop digital technologies to address shortages in healthcare.
source_quote: A political agreement was reached to strengthen the integration of healthcare services at the local level and quality assurance.

growth_orientation_rationale: The reforms are designed to stimulate R&D investment and innovation in the private sector, which is expected to enhance productivity and economic growth.
status_evidence: The reforms have been officially implemented as part of Denmark's ongoing efforts to boost R&D investment.
importance_rationale: The reforms significantly enhance existing tax incentives for R&D, which is a notable change but not a complete overhaul of the system.
description: Denmark has introduced favourable depreciation rules for green investment and increased the tax allowance for R&D expenses from 101.5% to 130%, with a DKK 50 million cap on qualifying expenditures. Additionally, plans are in place to further raise public support for research and development by making the R&D tax subsidy incremental, aiming to enhance incentives for innovation, particularly in small companies.
source_quote: The research and development tax subsidy remains relatively low by international standards. Further increase public support to research and development by raising the R&D tax subsidy and making it incremental.

growth_orientation_rationale: Fostering collaboration between academia and industry is expected to enhance innovation and commercialization of research, contributing to economic growth.
status_evidence: The funding allocation and initiatives have been officially implemented as part of Denmark's innovation strategy.
importance_rationale: The allocation of funds and the focus on collaboration represent a significant but not transformative change in policy.
description: Denmark allocated DKK 700 million to develop green research innovation partnerships, enhancing cooperation between firms and universities to foster innovation. Additionally, the government aims to raise incentives for cooperation between universities and businesses by providing targeted funding and revising intellectual property rights.
source_quote: Raise incentives for the cooperation between universities and businesses by providing targeted funding, revising intellectual property rights, and harmonising procedures.

growth_orientation_rationale: Enhancing access to capital for start-ups is expected to stimulate innovation and growth in the entrepreneurial sector.
status_evidence: The initiative has been officially announced and is set to be implemented in 2024.
importance_rationale: The additional funding represents a significant enhancement of support for start-ups, though it is not a complete overhaul of the system.
description: Denmark has prioritized an additional 6 billion DKK towards equity finance and access to capital in the scale-up phase, aiming to enhance support for innovative start-ups and SMEs.
source_quote: Denmark prioritised an additional 6 billion DKK towards equity finance and access to capital in the scale-up phase.

growth_orientation_rationale: Increasing access to higher education is expected to enhance the supply of skilled researchers and innovators, contributing positively to economic growth.
status_evidence: The agreement has been officially announced and is set to be implemented starting in 2024.
importance_rationale: The expansion of university places represents a significant step towards improving human capital, though it is not a complete overhaul of the education system.
description: Denmark's government has announced an agreement to increase the number of university places available for English-taught master's programmes, allowing for an additional 1,100 students each year from 2024-2028 and 2,500 from 2029.
source_quote: The recently announced agreement to allow universities to augment their enrolment of students in English-taught master’s programmes by 1,100 each year from 2024-2028, and by 2,500 from 2029 is welcome.

growth_orientation_rationale: Increasing R&D funding is expected to stimulate innovation and productivity in the business sector, contributing positively to economic growth.
status_evidence: The plan has been officially announced and is set to be implemented by 2025.
importance_rationale: This reform represents a significant increase in public funding for business R&D, marking a major policy shift.
description: Denmark plans to increase business R&D support by 0.25 percentage points of GDP by 2025, aiming to close the gap to the OECD average. This includes raising tax allowances for R&D expenses to 130% for incremental R&D expenses, thereby enhancing public support for business R&D.
source_quote: The government aims to increase business R&D by 0.25 ppt of GDP by 2025 to halve the gap to the OECD average.

growth_orientation_rationale: Improving productivity in the public sector through automation is expected to enhance efficiency and service delivery, contributing positively to economic growth.
status_evidence: The plan has been officially announced and is set to be implemented by 2025.
importance_rationale: The 10-year plan represents a significant structural change in how public services will be delivered.
description: Denmark is preparing a 10-year plan to achieve an additional 10,000 equivalent full-time employees in the public sector through automation and digitisation, which aims to improve productivity in the long-term care sector.
source_quote: Denmark is preparing a 10-year plan to achieve an additional 10,000 equivalent full-time employees in the public sector via automation and digitisation.

growth_orientation_rationale: Increasing the tax deduction for R&D expenditures incentivizes firms to invest more in research and development, which can enhance innovation and productivity.
status_evidence: The tax deduction increase was included in the business and entrepreneurship reform package.
importance_rationale: This reform is significant as it directly impacts the funding available for R&D activities, encouraging firms to enhance their innovative capabilities.
description: A business and entrepreneurship reform package was introduced, which includes a gradual increase in the tax deduction for R&D expenditures from 100% to 110% by 2026.
source_quote: Gradually increased tax deduction of R&D expenditures from 100% to reach 110% in 2026.

growth_orientation_rationale: Improving access to venture capital is expected to stimulate the growth of small and medium-sized enterprises, enhancing innovation and job creation.

importance_rationale: This proposal represents a significant shift in policy aimed at enhancing the financial support available to small businesses, which are crucial for economic dynamism.
description: A government committee proposed easing access to venture capital for small and medium-sized enterprises, allowing insurance companies and pension schemes to invest more in equity, and strengthening banks' capacity for venture capital investment.
source_quote: To encourage the growth of small and medium-sized enterprises, a government committee in mid-1995 proposed an easing of the access to venture capital for such companies.

growth_orientation_rationale: Lowering barriers for entrepreneurs is expected to stimulate innovation and economic activity.
status_evidence: The reform has been officially announced but not yet legislated.

description: Denmark aims to lower barriers for entrepreneurs and innovators to foster a more dynamic business environment, enhancing the startup ecosystem.
source_quote: The programme aims for: Lower barriers for entrepreneurs and innovators.

growth_orientation_rationale: Stimulating business-financed R&D is expected to enhance innovation capacity and productivity in the private sector.

description: The Danish government is stimulating business-financed research and development to enhance innovation capacity in the private sector.

description: The Danish government aims to raise research and development (R&D) investment to enhance productivity growth, focusing on improving framework conditions and promoting competition.
source_quote: Raising research and development (R&D) investment, currently close to the average EU level, could contribute to higher total factor productivity growth.

growth_orientation_rationale: Facilitating re-entry for failed entrepreneurs is expected to foster a more dynamic startup ecosystem and encourage innovation.
status_evidence: The proposal has been made but not yet implemented.

source_quote: The government put a proposal to parliament in late 2004 aimed at relaxing these regulations and making it easier for failed entrepreneurs to start over.

growth_orientation_rationale: Strengthening quality requirements for incubators is expected to enhance support for innovative startups.
status_evidence: The reform has been officially announced but not yet implemented.
importance_rationale: The reform is significant in improving the support structure for startups but is not a major overhaul.
description: Publicly funded business incubators will face stronger quality requirements, and firms undertaking substantial research will be allowed to compete for public research grants.
source_quote: Publicly funded business incubators should face stronger quality requirements.

growth_orientation_rationale: Increased spending on energy research fosters innovation and technological advancement in the energy sector.

status_evidence: The government has actively increased spending on energy research.
importance_rationale: The increase in funding represents a significant commitment to advancing energy technologies.
description: The Danish government has increased its spending on energy research to support the market maturation of existing technologies and the development of new technologies through the Energy Technology Development and Demonstration Programme.
source_quote: The Danish government has spent more and more on energy research in recent years.

growth_orientation_rationale: Improving the connection between academia and industry can lead to increased innovation and productivity in the economy.
status_evidence: The recommendation suggests a shift in funding strategy to enhance collaboration.
importance_rationale: This reform represents a significant shift in funding strategy that could enhance innovation capacity through better collaboration.
description: Denmark is moving towards a more balanced mix of project and institutional-based research funding to enhance links between universities and industry.
source_quote: Move towards a more balanced mix of project and institutional based research funding with the objective to increase the links between universities and industry.

description: In December 2012, the Danish government launched an Innovation Strategy that identified gaps in the innovation framework and proposed initiatives to enhance Denmark's position as an innovation leader, focusing on societal challenges and improving knowledge capacity.
source_quote: In December 2012, the government launched an Innovation Strategy. It identified some gaps in the innovation framework and proposed initiatives to help Denmark remain an innovation leader.

growth_orientation_rationale: Streamlining funding programmes can enhance efficiency and effectiveness in supporting innovation, potentially leading to increased economic growth.
status_evidence: The recommendation to evaluate and streamline innovation funding programmes was made.
importance_rationale: While significant, this reform is more about improving existing structures rather than creating new ones, thus it is important but not major.
description: The government is evaluating the recent merger of different innovation funding programmes to streamline innovation policy instruments and funding programmes.
source_quote: Evaluate the recent merger of different innovation funding programmes and if needed, consider further streamlining innovation policy instruments and funding programmes.

growth_orientation_rationale: By facilitating the market introduction of innovative solutions, the fund is expected to enhance competitiveness and drive economic growth.
status_evidence: The restructuring of the Business Innovation Fund was part of the announced Innovation Strategy.

description: The restructuring of the 'Business Innovation Fund' into a 'Market Development Fund' aims to support the introduction of innovative solutions to the market, including green and welfare solutions.
source_quote: Restructure the 'Business Innovation Fund' to turn it into a 'Market Development Fund' to support the introduction of innovative solutions to the market, including green and welfare solutions.

growth_orientation_rationale: Improving support for innovative firms can lead to increased productivity and competitiveness in the economy.
status_evidence: The recommendation emphasizes the need for better support mechanisms for innovative firms.
importance_rationale: This reform addresses a significant aspect of the innovation policy framework, focusing on enhancing support for a key segment of the economy.
description: Denmark aims to enhance the efficiency of its innovation policies by ensuring that some schemes provide effective support to young and dynamic innovative firms.

growth_orientation_rationale: Enhancing R&D tax credits can incentivize firms to invest more in research and development, leading to innovation and economic growth.
