importance_rationale: This reform is significant as it directly impacts the financial incentives for firms to engage in R&D activities.
description: The OECD recommends extending carry-over provisions and cash refunds in R&D tax credit programmes or increasing direct support to better assist young and dynamic firms.
source_quote: Further extend carry-over provisions and cash refunds in R&D tax credit programmes or increase direct support.

growth_orientation_rationale: Streamlining innovation policy instruments is expected to enhance efficiency and effectiveness in supporting innovation.
status_evidence: The reform has been officially announced but not yet legislated.
importance_rationale: The review aims to significantly improve the innovation funding landscape, indicating a substantial but not structural change.
description: The Danish government has initiated a review of the business development system, including the innovation system, to evaluate the recent merger of different innovation funding programmes and consider further streamlining innovation policy instruments and funding.
source_quote: The government has initiated a review of the business development system, including the innovation system.

growth_orientation_rationale: Increases overall business R&D investment, fostering innovation and competitiveness among smaller firms.
status_evidence: The government plans to broaden public support to business R&D.
importance_rationale: This reform is significant as it aims to diversify R&D funding sources and stimulate innovation across a broader range of firms.
description: Denmark is expanding its public support for business R&D through well-designed R&D grants and tax credits aimed at incremental R&D expenses, addressing the concentration of business R&D spending in a few large firms.
source_quote: Broaden public support to business R&D through well-designed R&D grants and tax credits for incremental R&D expenses.

growth_orientation_rationale: Enhances the availability of data for research and innovation, potentially leading to new services and business models.
status_evidence: The government aims to make more public data available to foster innovation.
importance_rationale: This initiative is important as it leverages existing public data to stimulate innovation and research, enhancing the overall innovation ecosystem.
description: The Danish government is investing in open access to public-sector basic data, including weather data, to foster innovation and research opportunities for businesses.
source_quote: Recent digital initiatives by the Government aim at fostering further open access to public-sector basic data.

growth_orientation_rationale: By reducing the tax bias against equity, the ACE could encourage more investment in innovative projects, thereby enhancing economic growth.
status_evidence: The Government has proposed the ACE as a reform to stimulate investment.
importance_rationale: The introduction of an ACE represents a significant shift in corporate tax policy that could fundamentally alter investment incentives and support innovation.
description: The Danish government is considering an allowance for corporate equity (ACE) to reduce the tax bias towards debt financing, which could stimulate investment and innovation.
source_quote: A more fundamental reform to introduce an allowance for corporate equity (ACE) is currently being considered by the Government.

growth_orientation_rationale: Facilitating collaboration is expected to enhance knowledge transfer and commercialization of research outputs.
status_evidence: The recommendation to improve collaboration between universities and businesses is emphasized in the survey.
importance_rationale: This reform is significant as it addresses barriers to collaboration, which is essential for enhancing innovation and economic growth.
description: The Danish government aims to improve collaboration between universities and businesses by simplifying the regulatory framework and enhancing intellectual property rights policies.
source_quote: Improve collaboration between universities and businesses by reducing the complexity of the system regulating cooperation, and improving intellectual property right policies of universities.

growth_orientation_rationale: Continued investment in wind energy supports renewable energy development and sustainability.
status_evidence: Ongoing funding and support for wind energy.
importance_rationale: The programme is a continuation of existing support for wind energy.
description: The Danish government has sustained support for wind research, development, and deployment, with significant subsidies and increasing funding throughout the 2000s, peaking at DKK 618 million in 2013.
source_quote: Sustained support for wind research, development and deployment, with significant subsidies in the late 1970s and 1980s and increasing funding throughout the 2000s, peaking at DKK 618 million in 2013.

growth_orientation_rationale: The strategy aims to enhance digital infrastructure and capabilities, supporting innovation and productivity.
status_evidence: Official announcement for 2022 launch.
importance_rationale: The strategy is a targeted initiative to improve digitalisation.
description: A new digitalisation strategy will be launched in early 2022 based on the Digitization Partnership recommendations.
source_quote: A new digitalisation strategy will be launched in early 2022 on the basis of the Digitization Partnership recommendations released in October 2021.

growth_orientation_rationale: The strategy aims to reduce emissions and promote sustainable practices in agriculture and industry.
status_evidence: Official announcements of investment plans.
importance_rationale: The strategy is significant for its focus on sustainable practices and emissions reduction.
description: Denmark is investing in research and development to enhance agriculture's contribution to a net-zero target, focusing on alternatives to greenhouse gas intensive practices and improving emissions monitoring. The green research strategy also emphasizes developing green fuels and Power-to-X technologies.
source_quote: In the longer term, research and development can contribute to provide alternatives to GHG intensive practices and therefore abate emissions with limited leakage effects.

growth_orientation_rationale: The plan supports green innovation and technology scaling, contributing to sustainable economic growth.
status_evidence: Official announcements of funding allocations.
importance_rationale: The plan provides significant funding for green R&D and innovation.
description: The Danish government announced a Green recovery plan, which includes substantial support for research and development of green technologies, with DKK 3,900 million allocated for this purpose. Additionally, a trans-partisan agreement allocated DKK 6 billion to the Danish Green Investment Fund to promote green innovation and scale up technologies in five key areas.
source_quote: A trans-partisan agreement between the Danish Government and other parties allocated DKK 6 billion to the Danish Green Investment Fund.

growth_orientation_rationale: The development of wind energy islands and charging infrastructure supports renewable energy and sustainable transport.
status_evidence: Official plans for infrastructure development.
importance_rationale: The plan involves large-scale infrastructure projects with significant investment.
description: The Danish government is planning to develop two wind energy islands to provide renewable electricity and sustainable fuels, with the first stage of the North Sea project estimated at EUR 29 billion. Additionally, a 10-year funding program for fast chargers on the trunk road network is part of the Infrastructure Plan 2035.
source_quote: The Danish government is planning to develop two wind energy islands, an artificial island in the North Sea and the Baltic island of Bornholm, to provide renewable electricity, sustainable fuels and interconnection with other countries.

growth_orientation_rationale: By facilitating new business creation, the scheme contributes to job creation and economic growth.
status_evidence: The Business Start-up scheme has been operational since its introduction.
importance_rationale: This reform represents a significant initiative to support entrepreneurship and reduce unemployment.
description: The Business Start-up scheme replaced the Enterprise Allowance Scheme in 1991, aimed at supporting new business creation and reducing unemployment.
source_quote: The Enterprise Allowance Scheme (EAS) was launched in 1983 and operated until its replacement in 1991 by the Business Start-up scheme.

growth_orientation_rationale: Enhancing skills and training is expected to improve workforce productivity and address skill shortages, thereby supporting economic growth.
status_evidence: The reform has been put into practice, with the Modern Apprenticeship initiative actively providing funding and training opportunities.
importance_rationale: The reform represents a significant overhaul of the educational and training system in the UK, aimed at addressing critical skill shortages and improving educational outcomes.
description: The United Kingdom launched a wide-ranging reform of educational and training institutions, including the Modern Apprenticeship initiative, which provides substantial public funding for craft and technician training. This initiative aims to enhance occupational training and address skill shortages in the UK labor market, while the broader reform seeks to improve the efficiency and effectiveness of educational provision through the establishment of a national curriculum and vocational training routes.
source_quote: The wide-ranging reform of educational and training institutions launched in the 1980s sought to improve the efficiency and effectiveness of educational provision.

growth_orientation_rationale: These initiatives are designed to stimulate investment and innovation in smaller firms, which can enhance productivity and economic growth.
status_evidence: The VCT and EIS are operational, and the Challenge Fund has been utilized for public funding bids.
importance_rationale: While these reforms are significant in promoting innovation, they do not represent a structural overhaul of the innovation system.
description: The VCT and EIS provide tax incentives for investments in smaller firms, while the Challenge Fund initiative encourages partnerships between public, private, and community sectors to bid for public funds aimed at local service development and regeneration, both aimed at stimulating growth and innovation in the private sector.
source_quote: The VCT and EIS both offer tax incentives for investment in smaller firms in the form of capital gains and income tax reliefs for investors.

growth_orientation_rationale: By enhancing efficiency and value for money, the PFI can lead to better public services and stimulate economic growth.
status_evidence: The PFI has been operational since its introduction.
importance_rationale: This reform represents a significant shift in public sector financing and service delivery.
description: The Private Finance Initiative (PFI) was introduced to shift the public sector's role from direct service provision to becoming a procurer and regulator, aiming to improve efficiency and value for money through private sector involvement.
source_quote: The central idea underlying PFI is a fundamental shift in the role of the public sector from being a direct provider of service towards becoming a procurer and a regulator for services that were once publicly provided.

growth_orientation_rationale: Lowering capital gains tax and adjusting tax reliefs encourages investment in innovative ventures, which can enhance economic growth.
status_evidence: The reform has been enacted and is currently in effect.
importance_rationale: The reform significantly alters the tax landscape for venture capital but does not constitute a major overhaul of the system.
description: The UK government has modified capital gains tax to lower rates on long-term investment and adjusted tax reliefs related to access to venture capital and high-risk investments, aiming to stimulate innovation and adaptability in the economy.
source_quote: The government has modified capital gains tax in the March 1998 Budget to lower rates on long-term investment and adjusted tax reliefs relating to access to venture capital and high-risk investments.

growth_orientation_rationale: By enhancing the skills and employability of disadvantaged groups, the programme supports the development of a more skilled workforce, which can benefit innovative firms.
status_evidence: The programme has been launched and is actively providing support.
importance_rationale: The programme represents a significant structural change in addressing unemployment and enhancing workforce skills.
description: The Welfare to Work programme aims to reduce youth and long-term unemployment by providing wage subsidies and training opportunities for disadvantaged groups, including young people and lone parents.
source_quote: The New Deal, or Welfare to Work initiative, is the first major element in the government’s employability strategy.

growth_orientation_rationale: Implementing a national minimum wage can improve worker productivity and economic stability, which supports innovation.
status_evidence: The reform has been publicly announced and is pending implementation.
importance_rationale: The introduction of a national minimum wage is a significant structural change in labor policy.
description: The UK government announced the introduction of a national minimum wage to address low pay and promote fairness in the workplace, with a Low Pay Commission established to recommend its level and design features.
source_quote: The Government is committed to introducing a national minimum wage to remove the worst excesses of low pay and ensure greater decency and fairness in the workplace.

growth_orientation_rationale: By promoting competition, the Act is expected to enhance market efficiency and innovation, leading to better outcomes for consumers and businesses.
status_evidence: The 1998 Competition Act has come into effect, aligning domestic rules with EU law.
importance_rationale: This reform represents a fundamental change in the UK's approach to competition policy, with significant implications for market dynamics and innovation.
description: The introduction of the 1998 Competition Act aims to enhance competition in the UK market by aligning domestic rules with EU law and encouraging whistle-blowing on anti-competitive practices.
source_quote: The 1998 Competition Act, which has just come into effect, largely remedies this weakness by aligning domestic rules on the prohibition of anti-competitive agreements.

growth_orientation_rationale: By addressing health inequalities and improving access to services, the initiative is expected to enhance overall public health and productivity.
status_evidence: 26 health action zones have been created and are operational.
importance_rationale: The initiative represents a significant effort to improve health services in deprived areas, with potential long-term benefits for public health and social equity.
description: The Government established 26 health action zones (HAZs) to target deprived areas with high incidences of ill health, aiming to modernize local health services through collaboration among local institutions.
source_quote: Since 1998, 26 health action zones (HAZs) have been created –for a period of seven years– covering a population of 13 million, in an attempt to target deprived areas with a particularly high incidence of ill health.

growth_orientation_rationale: The R&D tax credit encourages investment in innovation, which can lead to increased productivity and economic growth.
status_evidence: The tax credit was introduced as part of the March 1999 Budget.
importance_rationale: The introduction of the R&D tax credit represents a significant policy change aimed at enhancing innovation among SMEs, which is crucial for economic dynamism.
description: The March 1999 Budget introduced a tax credit for research and development (R&D) aimed at small and medium-sized enterprises (SMEs) that spend over £50,000 a year on R&D, thereby incentivizing innovation in the private sector.
source_quote: Corporate taxes for small enterprises were also cut via the introduction of a new 10 per cent rate for the smallest companies and a tax credit for research and development (R&D) for small and medium sized enterprises (SMEs) spending over £50,000 a year on R&D.

growth_orientation_rationale: The ILA scheme encourages skill development and vocational training, which can enhance workforce productivity and adaptability.
status_evidence: The Government introduced an individual learning account (ILA) scheme in April 1999.
importance_rationale: The ILA scheme represents a significant step towards promoting lifelong learning, although it is one of several initiatives aimed at improving skills in the workforce.
description: The Government introduced an individual learning account (ILA) scheme to promote lifelong learning, providing a £150 grant for vocational training to the first million individuals who open a special bank account.
source_quote: The Government introduced an individual learning account (ILA) scheme in April 1999.

growth_orientation_rationale: These initiatives are designed to stimulate entrepreneurship and innovation, which are critical for improving productivity and economic growth.
status_evidence: The Government has launched a wide range of initiatives to address the productivity challenge.
importance_rationale: This reform represents a significant shift in policy aimed at enhancing productivity through a comprehensive set of initiatives, addressing a critical economic challenge.
description: The Government has launched a wide range of initiatives aimed at addressing the longstanding productivity gap in the United Kingdom, which includes measures to enhance competition and entrepreneurial dynamism.
source_quote: The Government has launched a wide range of initiatives, big and small, to address them.

growth_orientation_rationale: These tax incentives are designed to lower the cost of doing business for SMEs, thereby encouraging investment in innovation and R&D activities.
status_evidence: The Government has committed to these tax changes in recent budgets.
importance_rationale: This reform represents a significant shift in the tax treatment of SMEs, aimed at fostering innovation and entrepreneurship, which is crucial for economic growth.
description: The UK Government has introduced a series of tax incentives aimed at boosting entrepreneurial activity, including a reduction in the corporate tax rate for small companies and enhanced capital allowances for R&D spending.
source_quote: The corporate tax rate for small companies has been cut from 23 to 20 percent for the basic rate, and a new entry rate of 10 percent has been introduced.

growth_orientation_rationale: By supporting business incubators and infrastructure, this fund aims to enhance the startup ecosystem, facilitating the growth of innovative firms and contributing to regional economic development.
status_evidence: The Government has announced the establishment of the fund to support regional innovation initiatives.
importance_rationale: This fund represents a significant investment in regional innovation capacity, although it is part of a broader strategy rather than a standalone major reform.
description: The UK Government has established a new £50 million Regional Innovation Fund to co-finance business incubators and small-scale infrastructure, aimed at fostering innovation in regional economies.
source_quote: The Government has announced it will create a new £50 million Regional Innovation Fund to enable RDAs to co-finance business incubators and small-scale infrastructure.

growth_orientation_rationale: By enhancing skills and providing financial support for job search, the initiative aims to improve employability and reduce long-term unemployment.
status_evidence: The Government announced the creation of Employment Zones in March 2000.
importance_rationale: The initiative represents a significant effort to address long-term unemployment through targeted support, although it builds on existing frameworks rather than introducing a completely new system.
description: The Government announced the creation of Employment Zones to provide intensified support for the long-term unemployed, combining funds from welfare and training programmes to offer Personal Job Accounts for training and job search costs.
source_quote: In March 2000, the Government announced the creation of Employment Zones, with a view to intensify the support provided under the New Deal to the long-term unemployed in unemployment black spots.

growth_orientation_rationale: The strategy aims to enhance the digital economy, which can lead to increased productivity and innovation in various sectors.
status_evidence: The Government has launched a comprehensive strategy to promote e-commerce.
importance_rationale: This reform represents a significant shift in policy aimed at transforming the UK's digital economy, with potential long-term impacts on innovation and competitiveness.
description: The UK Government launched a comprehensive strategy to promote e-commerce, aiming to make Britain the most favorable environment for e-commerce by 2002. This includes various tax breaks and subsidies to encourage IT spending and training.
source_quote: The Government’s ambitious objective of 'making Britain by 2002 the most favourable environment in which to conduct e-commerce'.

growth_orientation_rationale: The tax relief encourages firms to invest in R&D, which can lead to innovation, increased productivity, and ultimately economic growth.
status_evidence: The tax relief was introduced and later extended to large companies.
importance_rationale: The introduction of R&D tax relief represents a significant policy change aimed at enhancing innovation capacity among firms, particularly small businesses.
description: In 2000, the United Kingdom introduced a tax relief on R&D expenditure specifically for small firms, which was later extended to large companies in 2002. This reform aimed to incentivize research and development activities within the corporate sector.
source_quote: In 2000 a tax relief on R&D expenditure was introduced for small firms and later, in 2002, extended to large companies.

growth_orientation_rationale: By reducing the fear of failure, the reform encourages entrepreneurial activity and innovation.
status_evidence: The reform has been enacted to support entrepreneurship.
importance_rationale: The reform is significant for supporting entrepreneurship but does not represent a major structural change.
description: The reform of the Insolvency Law aims to distinguish between reckless managers and others, which is intended to support entrepreneurship by reducing the fear of failure.
source_quote: Innovation and entrepreneurship should not be impeded by fear of failure. The reform of the Insolvency Law, by distinguishing reckless managers from others, goes in the right direction.

growth_orientation_rationale: This reform is expected to improve efficiency in hospital funding, leading to better resource allocation and potentially enhancing healthcare outcomes.
status_evidence: The reform has been fully implemented since April 2003.
importance_rationale: The introduction of activity-based funding represents a major overhaul of the funding mechanism for hospitals.
description: The UK government began the phased introduction of activity-based funding for hospitals in April 2003, which allocates funding based on the activity levels of hospitals rather than historic costs.
source_quote: The NHS began the phased introduction of activity-based funding for hospitals in April 2003.

growth_orientation_rationale: By improving skills and training responsiveness, the reform aims to enhance employability and productivity in the workforce.
status_evidence: Both components of the reform have been implemented since 2003.
importance_rationale: The reforms represent significant but narrower changes aimed at improving skills and employment outcomes.
description: The UK government initiated a comprehensive framework for reform aimed at helping individuals on incapacity benefits return to work, focusing on addressing barriers such as health, financial issues, and lack of skills. Additionally, a Skills Strategy was presented to enhance training responsiveness to employer needs, allowing firms more control over publicly-funded training.
source_quote: In July 2003, the UK Government presented a Skills Strategy to make available training more responsive to the specific skill needs of employers and workers.

growth_orientation_rationale: Increasing R&D intensity is expected to drive innovation and productivity growth in the economy.
status_evidence: The government has set a target for R&D intensity as part of its long-term strategy.
importance_rationale: This ambitious target reflects a significant commitment to enhancing the UK's R&D landscape, which is critical for long-term economic growth.
description: The UK government aims to raise R&D intensity from around 1.7% of GDP in 2004 to 2.5% by 2014 as part of its 10-year plan for science and innovation.
source_quote: The government’s 10-year plan for science and innovation aims to raise R&D intensity from around 1.7% of GDP in 2004 to 2.5% by 2014.

growth_orientation_rationale: Increasing R&D intensity is expected to enhance productivity and innovation, contributing positively to economic growth.
status_evidence: The government has published the plan and set specific targets.
importance_rationale: This reform represents a significant structural change in the UK's approach to innovation, aiming to reverse a declining trend in R&D investment and enhance overall economic performance.
description: The UK government has published a ten-year plan aimed at boosting innovation performance, which includes a target to raise R&D intensity from 1.9% to 2.5% of GDP by 2014.
source_quote: The Government has recently published a 10-year plan for science and innovation which includes a target to raise R&D intensity from the current level of 1.9% to 2.5% of GDP by 2014.

growth_orientation_rationale: By improving the measurement of government output, the reform aims to enhance accountability and efficiency in public spending, potentially leading to better service delivery.
status_evidence: The final report was published in January 2005, establishing new measurement principles.
importance_rationale: This reform represents a significant methodological shift in how public sector productivity is measured, which can have far-reaching implications for public policy and resource allocation.
description: The Atkinson review has established new principles for measuring government output and productivity, leading to improved metrics in national accounts for public services, particularly in health and education.
source_quote: The Atkinson review has established a set of principles to apply within the internationally accepted national accounting frameworks.

growth_orientation_rationale: By enhancing vocational training, the reform aims to improve the skill set of the workforce, thereby increasing employability and productivity.
status_evidence: The white paper on education and skills was published in February 2005 outlining the reform.
importance_rationale: This reform represents a significant shift in vocational education, aiming to enhance the quality and relevance of vocational qualifications, which is crucial for addressing skills shortages in the economy.
description: The UK government announced a wide-ranging reform of vocational programmes for students aged 14-19, introducing specialist diplomas aimed at equipping young people with work-relevant skills and improving vocational education quality.
source_quote: The Government has announced a wide-ranging reform of vocational programmes for students aged 14-19 years.

growth_orientation_rationale: By improving access to higher education, the reform is expected to enhance the skills and employability of graduates, contributing positively to economic growth.
status_evidence: The reform has been officially announced but is not yet implemented.
importance_rationale: The introduction of a graduate contribution scheme represents a significant structural change in funding higher education.
description: The UK government plans to introduce a graduate contribution scheme allowing students to defer tuition fees until after graduation, addressing financial barriers to higher education. This scheme aims to resolve credit constraints for those from poorer backgrounds.
source_quote: The plan for a graduate contribution scheme launched in a recent white paper on 'The future of higher education' will deal with this problem by allowing all students to defer the payment of all fees until they have graduated.

growth_orientation_rationale: By enhancing the skills of the workforce, the programme aims to improve overall productivity and employability, addressing skill gaps in the labor market.
status_evidence: The programme is set to be established across England from 2006-07 following successful pilots.
importance_rationale: This initiative is a major reform aimed at addressing the skills shortage in the workforce, with significant implications for productivity and economic growth.
description: The National Employer Training Programme was announced to provide training for low-skilled workers, focusing on basic literacy skills and qualifications, with public funding covering training costs.
source_quote: The Government targets a 40% reduction in the number of workforce adults having less than the basic level 2 qualification.

growth_orientation_rationale: By increasing the R&D tax credits, the reform encourages firms to invest more in research and development, which can lead to innovation and productivity growth.
status_evidence: The increase in R&D tax credits was part of the March 2007 Budget.
importance_rationale: This reform represents a significant adjustment to existing tax incentives aimed at boosting R&D investment, which is crucial for fostering innovation in the economy.
description: The March 2007 Budget announced an increase in the R&D tax credits for small companies from 150% to 175% and for larger firms from 125% to 130%. This reform aims to enhance the incentives for R&D investment among businesses.
source_quote: There will be a new environmental tax credit and the rate of R&D tax credits for small companies will rise to 175% (from 150%); for larger firms they will increase from 125% to 130%.

growth_orientation_rationale: Improving adult education and skills is expected to enhance human capital, which is crucial for productivity and economic growth.
status_evidence: The government published its response to the Leitch Review, setting out new targets and initiatives.
importance_rationale: This initiative represents a significant overhaul of adult education policy aimed at addressing critical skills gaps in the UK workforce, with long-term implications for economic competitiveness.
description: The government commissioned the Leitch Review, which highlighted the UK's poor performance in adult learning and set new targets for education levels, including a literacy target of 95% by 2020 and increasing higher education qualifications to 40%.
source_quote: In July the government published its response, 'World Class Skills', in which it set out new targets across the range of education levels and made a number of announcements including greater employer involvement in the design and direction of training.

growth_orientation_rationale: Enhancing collaboration between universities and businesses can lead to increased innovation, knowledge transfer, and economic growth.
status_evidence: The government is providing £110 million a year support for business-university collaboration in England.
importance_rationale: This initiative is significant as it aims to strengthen the links between academia and industry, fostering innovation and enhancing the commercialization of research.
description: The UK government is promoting university-business collaboration by providing support through the Higher Education Innovation Fund and streamlining governance procedures.
source_quote: The government is providing £110 million a year support for business-university collaboration in England through the Higher Education Innovation Fund.

growth_orientation_rationale: By fostering a diverse childcare market, the reform encourages innovative solutions that can enhance service quality and accessibility, thereby supporting labor market participation.
status_evidence: The policy aim is to build a more coherent supply of childcare by 2008.
importance_rationale: This reform represents a significant structural change in childcare provision, aiming to enhance the quality and accessibility of services, which is crucial for supporting working families and promoting economic participation.
description: The UK government aims to develop a coherent supply of childcare by integrating public and private providers, which is expected to stimulate innovation in childcare services.
source_quote: The policy aim is to build a more coherent supply, as local authorities are given a duty to ensure sufficient childcare supply to meet the needs of families by 2008.

growth_orientation_rationale: By increasing funding per graduate, this reform is expected to improve educational quality and research output, contributing positively to the economy.
status_evidence: The changes to the higher education funding policy have been enacted and are currently in effect.
importance_rationale: This reform represents a significant overhaul of the higher education funding system, impacting the quality of education and research.
description: The UK government is changing higher education funding policy in England by increasing the cap on tuition fees to £9000 per year while reducing government grants to universities. This reform aims to ensure that universities have higher funding per graduate, which could enhance the quality of education and research output.
source_quote: The government is changing higher education funding policy in England, increasing the cap to £9000 per year, accompanied by changes to loans and grants to pay for tuition.

growth_orientation_rationale: By reducing borrowing costs for SMEs, the scheme aims to enhance their financial capacity to invest in innovation and growth.
status_evidence: The scheme has provided over £2.5 billion in loans to businesses.
importance_rationale: The scheme is a significant initiative to improve access to finance for SMEs, which is critical for fostering innovation.
description: The National Loan Guarantee Scheme was launched to help smaller businesses access affordable finance by providing government guarantees on unsecured borrowing.
source_quote: The National Loan Guarantee Scheme (NLGS) was launched on 20 March 2012 to help smaller businesses to access affordable finance.

growth_orientation_rationale: The increase in tax relief incentivizes SMEs to invest more in R&D, which can lead to greater innovation and productivity growth.
package_name: 2011 Innovation and Research Strategy for Growth
status_evidence: The rate of relief under the SME R&D tax credit scheme increased as planned.
importance_rationale: This reform represents a significant structural change in the support for R&D, enhancing the competitiveness of SMEs and potentially leading to substantial increases in R&D investment.
description: The Coalition Government increased support for R&D by SMEs through both tax and spending measures, raising the rate of relief under the SME R&D tax credit scheme to 225% of qualifying R&D costs from April 2012.
source_quote: From 1 April 2012, the rate of relief under the SME R&D tax credit scheme increases to 225% of qualifying R&D costs, one of the most competitive rates in the world.

growth_orientation_rationale: Investing in early education is expected to enhance human capital development, leading to better educational outcomes and long-term economic benefits.
status_evidence: The funding initiatives have been proposed and are set to be implemented by 2013.
importance_rationale: These reforms represent significant but narrower changes aimed at improving educational access and outcomes for disadvantaged children.
description: The UK government has set aside funding to extend the entitlement to fifteen hours per week of free early education to the 20% most disadvantaged two-year-olds in England by 2013, alongside introducing a pupil premium that provides additional funding to schools for each child entitled to free school meals. These initiatives aim to improve educational outcomes for disadvantaged children.
source_quote: The government has said that it wants a stronger focus on supporting the most vulnerable and disadvantaged families and increased use of evidence-based interventions within Sure Start Children’s Centres.

growth_orientation_rationale: By enabling the Green Investment Bank to borrow, the government aims to increase funding for green infrastructure projects, which can stimulate economic growth and innovation in sustainable technologies.
status_evidence: The government has considered giving the Green Investment Bank powers to borrow.
importance_rationale: This reform is significant as it aims to enhance funding for green infrastructure, which is essential for sustainable economic growth.
description: The government has considered giving the Green Investment Bank powers to borrow from 2015/16 to enhance investment in green infrastructure, which includes energy, water, transport, and waste.
source_quote: The government has considered giving the Green Investment Bank powers to borrow from 2015/16.

growth_orientation_rationale: By providing a stable framework for infrastructure investment, the NIC aims to enhance productivity and economic growth through better planning and prioritization of projects.
status_evidence: The NIC was established to support a stable framework for infrastructure investment.
importance_rationale: The creation of the NIC represents a fundamental shift in how the UK approaches infrastructure planning, aiming for a more integrated and strategic investment framework.
description: The UK government created the National Infrastructure Commission (NIC) to improve long-term infrastructure planning and investment across various sectors.
source_quote: The Government has recently set up the National Infrastructure Commission (NIC) in 2015, followed by the creation of the Infrastructure and Projects Authority (IPA) in 2016.

growth_orientation_rationale: Improving digital connectivity is expected to enhance productivity and economic activity across regions.
status_evidence: The government has committed to specific targets for broadband rollout.
importance_rationale: This initiative represents a significant investment in infrastructure that supports broader economic growth and innovation.
description: The UK government has pledged to deliver superfast broadband to 95% of households by 2017 and 97% by 2019, recognizing the importance of digital connectivity for economic development.
source_quote: The government has pledged to deliver superfast broadband for 95% of households by 2017 and 97% by 2019.

growth_orientation_rationale: The action plan aims to enhance access to finance for innovative firms, which can lead to increased productivity and economic growth.
status_evidence: Progress in implementation has been made since the introduction of the action plan.
importance_rationale: The plan represents a significant commitment to support innovative firms, which is crucial for long-term economic growth.
description: A 10-year action plan was introduced in 2017 to unlock over GBP 20 billion to finance growth in innovative firms, with progress made in implementation since then.
source_quote: A 10-year action plan was introduced in 2017 to unlock a total of over GBP 20 billion to finance growth in innovative firms.

growth_orientation_rationale: By facilitating pension fund investments in innovative firms, the reform aims to enhance the availability of capital for startups, which can drive innovation and economic growth.
status_evidence: The Government has taken measures to support occupational defined-contribution schemes in investing in innovative companies.
importance_rationale: This reform is significant as it opens up new funding avenues for innovative firms, which is essential for their growth and development.
description: The Government has taken measures to remove barriers for UK pension funds to invest in innovative companies, aiming to increase the financing pool available to young innovative firms.
source_quote: Since 2018, the Government has taken measures to remove barriers and support occupational defined-contribution (DC) schemes in investing in innovative companies.

growth_orientation_rationale: By increasing the number of apprenticeships, the reform aims to enhance the skills of the workforce, thereby improving productivity and economic growth.
status_evidence: The government has announced a very ambitious target of 3 million new apprenticeships by 2020.
